site.name: Morbi Rari
site.tagline: Centralized search for rare disease information

search.placeholder: Search a disease, a name, an ORPHA or OMIM code…
search.count: {count, plural, =0 {No results} one {1 result} other {# results}}
search.noResults: Nothing found. Check the spelling, or try another name for it.
search.degraded: Search is running in degraded mode: results may be less precise.

disease.noDefinition: This disease has no definition published in the source.
disease.retired: This entry is marked as retired in the original source.

dashboard.prevalenceHelp: Roughly how many people have it.
dashboard.geographyIntro: Reported frequency by country or region. This reflects where the disease has been studied, not only where it occurs: a missing country usually means missing studies.
dashboard.mapHint: Hover a country to see its figure. Use the wheel or the buttons to zoom, and drag to pan.
dashboard.phenotypesIntro: Manifestations described for this disease and how often they appear. This is information about the disease, not a diagnostic tool.
dashboard.genesIntro: Genes linked to this disease. Not all of them cause it: some only modify how it presents.
dashboard.subtypeGenesIntro: Orphanet does not link genes to this disease directly, but to its subtypes. These are the disease-causing genes described in each.
dashboard.whereToGoIntro: Centres actively researching this disease, who funds them and where they are. Drawn from clinical trials registered on ClinicalTrials.gov that are still open.
dashboard.trialsWarning: A clinical trial is research, not care. It has strict eligibility criteria, and taking part guarantees neither treatment nor benefit. Always talk to your doctor before contacting a centre.
dashboard.trialsAttribution: Source: ClinicalTrials.gov (U.S. National Library of Medicine). This site is not endorsed by the NIH. Trials are linked by MeSH code, so the list may be incomplete.
dashboard.drugsIntro: Medicines holding an orphan designation for this disease at regulatory agencies.
dashboard.drugsWarning: An orphan designation does NOT mean the drug is approved, available, or effective. It means an agency has recognised that it is being developed for a rare disease. Many designations never become a medicine.
dashboard.classificationIntro: Where this disease sits within Orphanet's medical classification.
dashboard.emptySection: The source publishes no data on this for this disease.

provenance.sourcesIntro: All information on this page comes from the public sources below. Each is responsible for its own data.

src.orphanet: Definition, classification, epidemiology, genes and synonyms
src.identifiers: Gene and protein identifiers (HGNC, Ensembl, UniProt, OMIM)

disclaimer.short: Reference information. Not medical advice, and not for diagnosis.
disclaimer.title: Important notice
disclaimer.bannerLead: Before you continue, please keep this in mind:
disclaimer.notAdvice: Morbi Rari does not provide medical advice and is not a substitute for consulting a healthcare professional.
disclaimer.noRelationship: Using this site does not create a doctor-patient relationship.
disclaimer.noDiagnosis: This site does not diagnose. It does not determine what condition a person has, nor suggest diagnoses from symptoms.
disclaimer.thirdParty: Information comes from third-party sources and may be outdated, incomplete, or contain errors.
disclaimer.consult: For any concern about your health, consult a qualified professional.
disclaimer.emergency: In an emergency, contact your local emergency services.
